I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand its structure and current state.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">find "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase" -type f -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" | head -50</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user said "Warmup" - this seems to be just an initial prompt to get me started. Let me explore the codebase to understand its structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">**/*.{ts,tsx}</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me explore the codebase structure to understand what we're working with.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase/"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me continue exploring the project structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase/src/"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Good, I can see the src directory structure. Let me get a better overview of the project.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cat "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase/package.json"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Now I have a good understanding of the project. This is a React + TypeScript project using Vite, with various dependencies for a knowledge base / dashboard application. Let me check the tasks directory to see if there's any active work.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase/tasks/" 2>/dev/null || echo "No tasks directory or empty"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me check the git log for recent context.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase" && git log -3 --oneline</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Good, I have context on the recent work. The project appears to be a personal dashboard/knowledge base application called "Neuman Brain Dashboard" based on the CLAUDE.md file. Recent commits show work on:
1. DeepSeek AI provider and terminal docs
2. Phantom Shell UI integration
3. Terminal rendering and AI Ghost Commands

Let me check the src structure more thoroughly.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">src/**/*.{ts,tsx}</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Now I have a comprehensive view of the codebase structure. This is a React application with:

1. **Pages**: Dashboard, Notes, Settings, Tasks, TimeTracking, Calendar, Ideas, LinkLibrary, etc.
2. **Components**: Various UI components including Header, Sidebar, modals, etc.
3. **Widgets**: Dashboard widgets for weather, tasks, notes, events, etc.
4. **Stores**: Zustand stores for state management
5. **Services**: AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek), weather, etc.
6. **Types**: TypeScript type definitions

The project follows a clean architecture with separation of concerns. Let me check the tasks directory for any pending work.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cat "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase/tasks/link-library-feature.md" 2>/dev/null || echo "File not found"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
There's an active task for Link Library feature. Let me read it to understand the current work in progress.
</thinking>

I've completed the warmup exploration. Here's a summary of the **Neuman Brain Dashboard** codebase:

## Project Overview

**Tech Stack:**
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
- Zustand 5 for state management
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- IndexedDB (via Dexie) for local storage
- Multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek)

## Directory Structure

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/pages/` | Main views (Dashboard, Notes, Tasks, Calendar, etc.) |
| `src/components/` | Reusable UI components (Header, Sidebar, modals) |
| `src/widgets/` | Dashboard widgets (Weather, Tasks, Notes, Events) |
| `src/stores/` | Zustand state stores |
| `src/services/` | AI providers, weather, sync services |
| `src/types/` | TypeScript interfaces and types |
| `src/hooks/` | Custom React hooks |
| `docs/` | Technical and product documentation |
| `tasks/` | Active task files |

## Recent Work
- DeepSeek AI provider integration
- Phantom Shell terminal UI (5 phases complete)
- Link Library feature (in progress)

## Active Task
There's a `link-library-feature.md` task file in the tasks directory, indicating ongoing work on a bookmark/link management feature.

## Git Status
Multiple files modified including new components like `CollectionSidebar.tsx`, `LinkLibrary.tsx`, and various stores. Several new utility files for bookmark parsing and color management.

Ready for your next instruction.
